source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < all_names.length; i++)
what_input_feature: A name-table header whose count/stringOffset fields and trailing NameRecord array survive hb_sanitize_context_t so that all_names.length (= this->table->count) is non-zero; the structured header tags ('typ1', 'name', table fourccs like 'GB') must match the values the parser compares against.
why_winner_satisfies: The VPC winner-resolving seed begins with structured header bytes 74 79 70 31 ('typ1') and contains 'name' and a fourcc-like 'GB' run, and resolves the branch (resolved=9/10) hitting the loop body 13 times on average; its lineage carries an I2S-floor dash row, evidence that I2SRandReplace substituted a logged CMP operand (a magic/fourcc constant) into the header so sanitization passed.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser blocks 10/10 with avg hitcount 0 — its seeds never produce a sanitization-passing non-zero count; value_profile has no I2SRandReplace, so it cannot copy the exact compared header constant into the input and stays stuck at the false (zero-length) side.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog has the I2SRandReplace mutator that reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the other operand of an intercepted integer/string CMP at matching offsets, planting the exact header constant the name-table sanitizer demands; plain value_profile lacks this mutator, so this pair's win is attributable to I2S.

source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < all_names.length; i++)
what_input_feature: Incrementally improving header bytes that approach a valid count/offset relationship; the CMP_MAP gradient rewards inputs whose compared operands get closer to matching, providing intermediate corpus stepping stones toward a sanitization-passing non-zero NameRecord count.
why_winner_satisfies: The VPC winner resolves 9/10 vs cmplog's 1/10 and reaches 13 hits vs 2; VPC adds the CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback on top of cmplog's I2S, so partial-match header states are preserved as corpus and combined with I2S substitution to clear the sanitizer.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 9/10 with avg hitcount only 2 — it has I2S but no CMP_MAP gradient, so near-miss header inputs that improve operand-distance are not retained as new coverage and the search has no gradient toward a valid non-zero-count table.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP records (PC, operand-distance bucket); an input whose CMP operands are closer to matching than any seen adds a new bucket and is kept as corpus, giving a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient toward a passing header that plain cmplog (no CMP_MAP) lacks; this pair's win is attributable to value_profile.

source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < this->names.length; i++)
what_input_feature: A name-table header whose magic/structural tags (the 'name' sfnt tag and NameRecord platform/encoding/nameID/language CMP fields) match the values harfbuzz tests at parse time, so all_names is non-empty and at least one entry survives into names.length, making the line-504 loop take its true side.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner seed carries literal sfnt-table tags ('typ1', 'name', and GB/G tag bytes) at the table-directory offsets, populating NameRecords so names.length becomes 12 and the loop iterates; the winner resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 12 on the branch.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser never plants those exact structural tags; its name table parses to zero usable records, names.length stays 0, and the loser blocks 10/10 with avg hitcount 0 on the branch.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds where the program compared the input against the expected sfnt tag / NameRecord constants, and substitutes those exact constants into the input — value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot place the exact tag bytes, so vpc beats vp on this axis.

source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < this->names.length; i++)
what_input_feature: The same structural name-table fields, but reached incrementally: byte sequences progressively closer to the expected platform/encoding/language constants so that near-matching inputs are retained and refined into a fully-parseable record set with names.length>0.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner accretes the right structural bytes; against plain cmplog the winner resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 12 versus the loser's 1/10 and hitcount 2, the extra gain coming from CMP_MAP gradient retention of partial matches.
why_loser_doesnt: Plain cmplog has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partially-correct intermediates are not preserved as corpus and the path to a complete parseable record set is not climbed; cmplog blocks 9/10 with avg hitcount only 2.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching the expected name-table constants, letting near-matching intermediates be refined toward names.length>0; plain cmplog lacks this gradient retention, so vpc beats cmplog on this axis.

what_input_feature: A 4-byte 'CFF2' table tag (0x43 0x46 0x46 0x32) present at the table-directory entry (offset 0x0c in the winning seeds) so that reference_table<cff2> resolves to a real table rather than the Null pool.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal ASCII tag 'CFF2' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f (43 46 46 32) which the directory parser matches to find the table; the false branch (cff2 != Null) and downstream top-dict parsing are then exercised.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile-blocking seeds carry different bytes at 0x0c-0x0f (e.g. 47 50 4f 53 = 'GPOS' / other tags) so reference_table<cff2> returns the Null sentinel, the true branch is taken, and execution jumps to fail.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver tag-equality compare and substitutes the required 'CFF2' literal into the seed at the matching offset; value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and only buckets Hamming distance, so it cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte tag — hence vpc beats vp.

what_input_feature: A table-directory and CFF2 header byte layout (offset/length fields around 0x00-0x1f) progressively shaped toward a sanitizable structure, where partial-match progress on multi-byte offset/length compares earns corpus retention.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds show structured header fields (e.g. 00 01 00 00 00 01 sfnt version and offset bytes 00 00 00 18 / 02) that the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient rewards as it approaches a valid directory, letting the winner cross to the false branch within 7.10h.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog-blocking seeds remain blocked (9/10) for 22.20h because cmplog has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback; without distance-bucket coverage credit it cannot incrementally retain near-valid header layouts, so the directory/offset structure is never assembled even when the tag is solvable.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient grants new-coverage credit for inputs whose offset/length operands are Hamming-closer to a sanitizable layout, steering the corpus toward the valid structure; cmplog lacks this feedback, so vpc (which has both) beats cmplog.

function: hb-ot-font.cc:hb_ot_get_glyph_from_name(hb_font_t*, void*, char const*, int, unsigned int*, void*)
what_input_feature: The literal 4-byte table tag 'post' (70 6f 73 74) placed at the table-directory offset (here 0x0c-0x0f), so the OpenType loader recognizes and parses a post table whose name lookup can succeed.
why_winner_satisfies: All three winner-resolving seeds contain the exact ASCII run 'post' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f (70 6f 73 74) plus the 'true' magic at 0x00-0x03, and value_profile_cmplog resolves the true branch in 8/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds carry no 'post' tag at 0x0c-0x0f (they show 'kern'/'fff'/'erdn' style bytes there) and resolve the branch 0/10, because plain value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace to substitute the exact 'post' FOURCC into the directory offset.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the table-tag comparison against the literal 'post', and substitutes that operand into the seed at the directory offset; value_profile has no such substitution stage so it cannot place the exact 4-byte tag.

function: hb-ot-font.cc:hb_ot_get_glyph_from_name(hb_font_t*, void*, char const*, int, unsigned int*, void*)
what_input_feature: Beyond the 'post' tag, the input must supply post-table substructure (version/name-index/string bytes) close enough to a valid name entry that get_glyph_from_name returns a glyph; partial-match feedback is needed to incrementally home in on it.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog carries CMP_MAP gradient buckets that reward inputs whose post-table CMP operands move closer to matching, and it resolves the branch 8/10 with avg hitcount 4 on the branch.
why_loser_doesnt: Plain cmplog has I2SRandReplace but no CMP_MAP partial-match feedback, so it cannot preserve incremental progress toward a valid name entry and resolves only 1/10 trials (blocked 9).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming-distance / matching-prefix buckets) preserves corpus inputs that get closer to a satisfying post-table name comparison; cmplog lacks this feedback so partial progress is discarded between executions.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte table-directory tag must be the literal FOURCC 'OS/2' (bytes 4f 53 2f 32) so the OS/2 table is parsed and OS2->use_typo_metrics() && OS2->has_data() resolve true at line 82.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed places the exact ASCII run 'OS/2' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, the table tag harfbuzz compares against when locating the OS/2 table; the winner's ancestor chain carries an I2S-floor dash row.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser lacks I2SRandReplace, so it never substitutes the exact 'OS/2' magic tag and resolved=0/10 at the branch; its blocking seeds show no 'OS/2' tag at the directory offsets.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver tag comparison and substitutes the exact 'OS/2' FOURCC operand into the input, an operation value_profile cannot perform because it has no CmpLog buffer / I2S stage.

what_input_feature: Beyond the exact tag, the OS/2 table fields (sTypoAscender / use_typo flags and table length offsets) must be filled to multi-byte values that pass has_data() and the typo-metrics gate, requiring incremental refinement of the structured byte fields.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient rewards inputs whose OS/2 length/offset and flag comparisons get progressively closer, so it climbs to a structurally valid table (winner hitcount=4, resolved=9/10).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has no CMP_MAP distance feedback, so once the tag matches it cannot incrementally tune the remaining OS/2 length/flag fields; it resolved only 1/10 and avg hitcount=0.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback preserves intermediate inputs that bring the OS/2 structural comparisons closer to matching, a gradient cmplog lacks, enabling the extra refinement past the bare tag match.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte post-table version field must equal the exact 32-bit constant 0x00020000 (bytes 00 02 00 00) at the version offset, so the equality test version != 0x00020000 fails and the function continues into v2 parsing instead of returning.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries the version bytes (00 02 00 00 at offset 0x17-0x1a window: 18 00 02 00 00), and the winner resolves 10/10 with hitcount 39 on the branch; the I2S stage in value_profile_cmplog substitutes the logged comparison operand 0x00020000 into the input.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile loser seeds do not place 0x00020000 at the version field (byte-diff offsets 0x17/0x19 show W=18/02 vs scattered L bytes), so version != 0x00020000 stays true and it blocks 10/10.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which reads the CmpLogObserver entry for the version != 0x00020000 comparison and substitutes the constant operand 0x00020000 directly into the input; plain value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and only gets a coarse CMP_MAP distance bucket, which gives no gradient on an all-or-nothing 4-byte equality.

what_input_feature: The same 4-byte version field must reach 0x00020000; here the discriminating capability is the CMP_MAP closeness gradient that preserves intermediate inputs whose version operand is progressively nearer to the constant.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 10/10 (hitcount 39) versus cmplog's 2/10 (hitcount 3); the winner's extra CMP_MAP gradient bucket signal preserves near-miss version inputs as corpus, accelerating convergence to the exact 0x00020000 magic.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2SRandReplace but no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so it only resolves 2/10 and blocks 8/10 with avg duration 19.80h, lacking the closeness-bucket signal that rewards inputs approaching the constant.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (Hamming-distance buckets on the integer compare) gives new coverage credit to inputs whose version operand is closer to 0x00020000, retaining stepping-stones cmplog discards; this distance-bucket feedback is the technique cmplog lacks.

what_input_feature: A 4-byte field at offset 0x18 holding the big-endian value 00 02 00 00 (0x00020000), the post-table version 2.0, matched exactly by the gate at line 223.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the bytes 00 02 00 00 at offsets 0x18-0x1b; the byte-diff shows offset 0x18 W=00x5, 0x19 W=02x5, 0x1a W=00x5, 0x1b W=00x5, exactly the 0x00020000 the version==0x00020000 gate requires.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot copy the intercepted 0x00020000 CMP operand into the input; its loser-blocking seeds carry arbitrary bytes at offset 0x18 (e.g. 0x1b loser=47(G)x8) and never satisfy the version-2.0 gate, blocking on the false side.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer for the version==0x00020000 comparison and substitutes the recorded constant 0x00020000 at the matching input offset, which value_profile (no I2SRandReplace) cannot do.

what_input_feature: The same 4-byte version field at offset 0x18 must reach the 32-bit constant 0x00020000 checked by the line-223 gate.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient preserves intermediate inputs whose version field is progressively closer (smaller Hamming distance) to 0x00020000, and the winner resolves 10/10 with hitcount 39 on the branch versus cmplog's 2/10 and hitcount 3.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog alone has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partial-match version values are not retained as corpus and the search lacks the distance-bucket hill-climb; cmplog blocks 8/10 with avg duration 19.80h on this branch.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a (PC, operand-distance bucket) for the version==0x00020000 comparison; an input closer to 0x00020000 adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus, giving the vpc winner a gradient toward the constant that bare cmplog lacks.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte post-table version field must equal the exact magic 0x00020000; the winner seeds carry version 0x0002 (bytes 00 02) where the loser seeds carry other values.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with the post-table header and shows the byte run 00 02 00 00 at the version offset (0x18 region: '00 02 00 00 00 0a'), so version != 0x00020000 is false and the OR short-circuit does not fire.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds carry no I2S substitution stage, so they never copy the intercepted 0x00020000 comparison operand into the version offset; their byte sets at 0x18 are dominated by 00 and unrelated values, leaving version != 0x00020000 true.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace intercepts the version-vs-0x00020000 CMP, finds the input bytes matching one operand, and substitutes the literal 0x00020000 into the input; value_profile lacks this mutator (I2S delta).

what_input_feature: Beyond matching the version magic, the glyph index must be brought in bounds (glyph < glyphNameIndex->len), which requires the CMP_MAP gradient feedback to preserve near-matching length/index comparisons.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner hits the false side 55089 times and proceeds into the array-indexing body (lines 258-272 marked [W]), indicating the glyph-vs-len bound was satisfied repeatedly under the combined CMP_MAP gradient signal.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 2/10 with avg hitcount 1820 versus the winner's 55089; without value_profile's CMP_MAP bucket feedback it cannot retain inputs that incrementally close the glyph>=glyphNameIndex->len bound comparison.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records (PC, operand-distance bucket) so an input closer to matching the bound is preserved as corpus; cmplog lacks this gradient feedback (value_profile delta).

what_input_feature: A well-formed sfnt header (00 01 00 00) plus a table directory containing cmap/GSUB so the font parses and get_nominal_glyph(0x0020) resolves; the magic 4-byte table tags ('cmap','GSUB') must appear verbatim.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal sfnt magic and table tags ('cmap', 'GSUB'/'GSUC') at fixed offsets, so the font directory parses and the U+0020 nominal-glyph lookup succeeds, taking the true side.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile loser seeds are short/garbled (17-79 bytes) without the intact sfnt header and table tags, so the font never exposes a glyph for 0x0020 and the branch stays false (resolved=0/10).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer from the font-table-tag CMPs and substitutes the exact tag/magic bytes (e.g. 'cmap', sfnt version) into the input, satisfying the structural gate that pure value_profile (no I2SRandReplace) cannot place exactly.

what_input_feature: Beyond the parseable font, the byte stream must drive a unicode-space codepoint whose space_fallback_type != NOT_SPACE while the cmap actually maps 0x0020, so line 196 reaches its true side.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner adds CMP_MAP gradient feedback: it preserves intermediate inputs that bring CMP operands progressively closer, so it climbs the multi-condition gate (space_type check at L195 plus the 0x0020 lookup at L196) that cmplog's discrete I2S substitution alone stalls on.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (no value_profile) gets no Hamming/prefix gradient, so it cannot incrementally tune the codepoint/table bytes toward the space-fallback condition and resolves only 1/10 with hitcount 0 on the branch.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient buckets reward inputs whose operands are closer to matching, giving a smooth path to the conjoined space_type and 0x0020-glyph conditions that cmplog's edge-only feedback lacks.

what_input_feature: A font blob whose table directory carries valid 4-byte FOURCC table tags (e.g. 'cmap', 'GSUB') at the directory offsets, so the shaper parses real tables and reaches the buffer state that sets the HAS_SPACE_FALLBACK scratch flag.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile_cmplog winner-resolving seeds contain literal ASCII table tags 'cmap' (offset 0x0c-0x0f: 63 6d 61 70) and 'GSUC'/'GSUB' (offset 0x1c-0x1f: 53 55 43/42) in the table directory, i.e. exact 4-byte tag operands I2SRandReplace can place by reading intercepted FOURCC comparisons.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile loser-blocking seeds are short and lack the well-formed tag directory at these offsets (e.g. offset 0x0c is 47(G)x9 / mixed and offset 0x1c is 00(.)x5/mixed), so they never reach the shaping state that sets the space-fallback flag and the true branch stays blocked (0/10 resolved).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog has I2SRandReplace (cmplog stage) which value_profile lacks; I2S reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, matches the input against an intercepted FOURCC operand, and substitutes the exact 4-byte table tag, letting the parser accept the table directory and drive the shaper to set the space-fallback flag.

what_input_feature: Multi-byte structural fields (table offsets / lengths / counts in the font directory) whose values must be progressively nudged toward valid magnitudes for the parser to accept the table and reach the space-fallback state.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog also has the CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partially-correct structural-field bytes (closer-matching operand pairs) are preserved as corpus and refined; the winner reaches the branch (avg hitcount on branch winner=7) where plain cmplog does not.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog lacks the CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback, so near-miss structural values give no coverage credit and are discarded; the cmplog loser resolves only 1/10 trials and shows avg hitcount on branch loser=0, never sustaining the partial progress needed to reach the flag.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog has the CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile stage) which cmplog lacks; the Hamming/prefix-distance bucket rewards inputs whose CMP operands get closer to matching, preserving intermediate font-structure states as corpus so the parser eventually accepts the layout and the space-fallback flag is set.

what_input_feature: A run of bytes whose use_category() values form the exact glyph-category prefix the USE machine needs to reach the case-10 (use_sakot_terminated_cluster) transition; the winner seeds carry concentrated 0x25/0x16/0x06 category runs at offsets 0x00-0x23.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog preserves intermediate inputs whose CMP-operand distance to the required category-key boundary shrinks, climbing the _keys[0] <= use_category() <= _keys[1] range comparison via CMP_MAP gradient buckets; the winner resolves the case-10 true side (W tag at line 981, T=4 in DIVERGENT BRANCHES).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has the I2S substitution stage but no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partial-progress inputs toward the category-range key are not retained as corpus and the case-10 transition is never reached (loser blocked=10/10, T=0 at line 981).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/range-distance bucket feedback on the _keys[0] <= use_category() <= _keys[1] comparison at lines 966-967) keeps near-miss category sequences alive; this is the technique cmplog lacks, so it is the axis distinguishing this pair.

what_input_feature: Specific glyph-category code bytes (e.g. the USE category literals in the 0x25/0x16/0x05 run) placed at the offsets the syllable machine reads, so the trans-action table dispatches to case 10.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted use_category() range-comparison operands and substitutes the needed category-key byte directly into the input; the winning seed 5542b009878a902b carries an I2S-floor dash-row ancestor (depth 20), and resolves the case-10 branch.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace mutator, so it cannot directly stamp the exact category-key byte the comparison wants and must rely on slow havoc drift; the loser stays blocked 9/10 (T=0 at line 981).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitution copies the intercepted category-comparison operand into the input at matching offsets, the technique value_profile lacks; this is the axis distinguishing this pair.

what_input_feature: A font/tag input that drives the parsed script value to the HB_SCRIPT_INVALID sentinel (the switch operand at line 41 must equal the INVALID constant), reaching the case at line 43.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 10/10; its winning seeds carry an I2S-floor (mutation_op = -) ancestor in lineage, indicating I2SRandReplace substituted the operand bytes that steer script to the INVALID/default-script comparison target.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, so it must blindly mutate the bytes feeding the script value; it resolves only 2/10 and stays blocked 8/10 with avg blocked duration 22.10h.
mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace (cmplog input-to-state): the CmpLogObserver records the script-vs-constant comparison operands and the mutator substitutes the matching operand into the input, the technique value_profile lacks but value_profile_cmplog adds.

what_input_feature: The same INVALID-script reaching input, but arrived at by progressively closing the multi-byte distance on the chained tag/language comparisons (lang_matches, parse_private_use_subtag) that gate which script value is produced.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient feedback; it preserves intermediate inputs that get the chained tag comparisons closer to matching, so it resolves 10/10 where cmplog (no CMP_MAP) resolves only 2/10.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partial-progress inputs on the lang/tag comparison chain are not retained as corpus; cmplog stays blocked 8/10 with avg blocked duration 19.90h.
mechanism_attribution: CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile Hamming/prefix-distance feedback): new CMP_MAP buckets for closer-matching operand pairs preserve intermediate inputs, the technique cmplog lacks but value_profile_cmplog adds.

what_input_feature: A well-formed font blob whose header/table-directory bytes (offsets 0x00-0x1f) carry recognized 4-byte table tags such as 'GSUB' / 'post' so the harness descends into hb_ot_tags_from_script_and_language and actually reaches the blocker at line 174.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry literal ASCII table tags at the head ('GSUB' at 0x0c-0x0f in seed 1/2, 'post' in seed 3/4), the exact 4-byte FOURCC magic an I2S CMP wants substituted; the dash-row ancestor in the lineage confirms an I2SRandReplace find planted such a tag.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot copy a CMP operand (a 4-byte FOURCC) verbatim into the table-directory offsets; its loser-blocking seeds (e.g. seed 1 'y-hant-hk') never form the recognized table tags and stall taking the true branch 8/10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes a logged 4-byte FOURCC operand at the matching input offsets, planting the exact table tag that value_profile's blind havoc cannot synthesize.

what_input_feature: A script/language subtag string whose old-script-tag lookup (hb_ot_old_tag_from_script) returns HB_OT_TAG_DEFAULT_SCRIPT, i.e. a near-match language tag refined byte-by-byte toward a value that makes old_tag equal the default.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry language-like subtags ('uu-hans', '-u-hans') in the tail, the product of incremental gradient refinement; cmplog's I2S alone cannot climb such a multi-byte similarity gradient, but value_profile's CMP_MAP rewards each byte that brings the lookup operands closer.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog lacks the CMP_MAP gradient, so it gets no intermediate credit for partial-prefix language tags and cannot iteratively steer old_tag toward the default; it blocks 8/10 with no preserved gradient stepping stones.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile/vpc CMP_MAP gradient buckets reward inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching, preserving partial-prefix language tags as corpus so the lookup is incrementally steered to return the default script tag.

function: OT::CPALV1Tail::get_palette_name_id(void const*, unsigned int, unsigned int) const
what_input_feature: The input must carry the literal CPAL FOURCC tag (bytes 43 50 41 4c) in the table directory so the CPAL parser is selected, then a CPALV1Tail with a non-zero paletteLabelsZ offset so the false branch at CPAL.hh:63 is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winning seed contains the literal 'CPAL' tag at offsets 0x0c-0x0f and reaches line 64 ([W]), the dereference past the !paletteLabelsZ guard; its ancestor chain has an I2S-floor dash row, so I2SRandReplace substituted the exact CPAL magic.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it never substitutes the exact 4-byte CPAL tag; its blocking seeds carry unrelated bytes (e.g. 47(G)/4f(O)/53(S) at 0x0c-0x0f) and stay on the true branch (T=20 F=0).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact CPAL FOURCC operand into the input, which the havoc-only value_profile cannot synthesize; this is the I2S delta that distinguishes vpc from value_profile.

function: OT::CPALV1Tail::get_palette_name_id(void const*, unsigned int, unsigned int) const
what_input_feature: Beyond the CPAL tag, the CPAL v1 header offset/length fields must be tuned so paletteLabelsZ resolves to a non-zero sub-table offset; reaching this multi-field constraint benefits from incremental Hamming/prefix gradient feedback.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner's header bytes (offsets 0x17=1c, 0x19=ff, 0x1b=04, 0x1c=73) are concentrated/structured distinct from losers; vpc's CMP_MAP gradient preserves intermediate inputs whose offset comparisons edge closer to a valid non-zero paletteLabelsZ.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partially-correct header offsets that do not yet flip a branch are discarded; cmplog blocks 10/10 at the branch (resolved=0/10 blocked=10).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records (PC, operand-distance bucket); an input whose CPAL offset/length comparison is closer to a non-zero paletteLabelsZ adds a new bucket and is kept as corpus, giving the gradient hill-climb that pure cmplog lacks. This is the value_profile delta distinguishing vpc from cmplog.

function: OT::CPALV1Tail::get_color_name_id(void const*, unsigned int, unsigned int) const
what_input_feature: The table-directory entry must carry the exact 4-byte FOURCC 'CPAL' (43 50 41 4c) at the tag offset so the CPAL parser is dispatched; the winner seed has 'CPAL' at bytes 0x0c-0x0f.
why_winner_satisfies: The winning vpc seed contains the literal 'CPAL' magic (43 50 41 4c) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, satisfying the 4-byte tag CMP that selects the CPAL parse path en route to colorLabelsZ.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so its blocking seeds carry mismatched tag bytes (e.g. 47 4f 53 = 'GOS' fragment at 0x0c-0x0f) and never land the exact 'CPAL' FOURCC; it stays blocked 10/10.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted 4-byte tag CMP from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected 'CPAL' operand at the matching input offset; value_profile has no such mutator, so it cannot synthesize the exact FOURCC.

function: OT::CPALV1Tail::get_color_name_id(void const*, unsigned int, unsigned int) const
what_input_feature: Beyond the CPAL tag, the CPAL v1 header fields (version, numColors/numPalettes, and the colorLabelsZ offset) must form a self-consistent multi-byte structure where colorLabelsZ resolves non-zero; this needs a gradient walk over several near-miss integer fields.
why_winner_satisfies: The winning vpc seed reaches the false branch with hitcount 2 and structured header bytes (00 01 00 00 00 01 ... 00 00 00 1c) consistent with a parsed v1 CPAL whose offset fields resolve to a non-zero colorLabelsZ.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partial-match intermediate inputs for the multi-field offset structure are not preserved as corpus; it never accumulates the near-match progression and stays blocked 10/10 with hitcount 0.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile/vpc's CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets so inputs that bring the colorLabelsZ-related offset fields closer to a valid non-zero value are kept and refined; cmplog (I2S-only, no CMP_MAP) lacks this gradient and cannot ratchet the structure into validity.

function: OT::CPAL::get_palette_colors(unsigned int, unsigned int, unsigned int*, unsigned int*) const
what_input_feature: The input must carry the exact font-table magic FOURCCs at fixed offsets: '_COLR' (5f 43 4f 4c 52) at 0x1b-0x1f and the 'hcts'-style tag bytes at 0x14-0x17, so that the harfbuzz table directory parses and palette_index stays < numPalettes to reach a populated CPAL table.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds (value_profile_cmplog) contain the literal '_COLR' run at 0x1b-0x1f and the c/t/s tag bytes at 0x15-0x17; the byte diff shows these offsets are concentrated to a single W value (e.g. 5f(_)x10, 43(C)x10, 4f(O)x10, 4c(L)x10, 52(R)x10).
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds carry language-tag/text bytes ('-hant-hk', 'zh-hant', 'jban') and never place the '_COLR' FOURCC at 0x1b-0x1f; at those offsets the L byte set is dominated by 00 with no concentrated _COLR run.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the harfbuzz table-tag comparison operands, and substitutes the exact expected FOURCC (e.g. _COLR) at the matching input offsets — value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot place the literal magic bytes deterministically.

function: OT::CPAL::get_palette_colors(unsigned int, unsigned int, unsigned int*, unsigned int*) const
what_input_feature: Beyond the magic tags, the input must drive the multi-field CPAL/COLR header (version, numPalettes, colorRecordIndices, offsets) close enough that palette_index < numPalettes and the table validates — a multi-byte numeric gradient the fuzzer must climb.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds show a structured header region (00 01 00 00 00 02 ... 00 09 ... 00 08) at 0x00-0x12 that the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient can incrementally tune; the branch is hit 220 times on the winner (avg hitcount=220) vs 0 on the loser.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser, lacking CMP_MAP feedback, gets no Hamming/prefix-distance reward for partially-correct multi-byte header fields, so it never preserves the intermediate near-match corpus needed to climb to a valid palette_index; loser resolved=0/10 blocked=10.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a coarse operand-distance bucket per CMP, rewarding inputs whose header fields get progressively closer to the validating values; cmplog (without CMP_MAP) sees only edge buckets and gets no gradient on the multi-field numeric checks.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte ASCII tag 'CPAL' (43 50 41 4c) must appear at offset 0x0c so the font table directory entry matches the CPAL FOURCC the parser looks up; this is an exact 4-byte literal match.
why_winner_satisfies: All four winner-resolving seeds carry 43 50 41 4c ('CPAL') at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, the FOURCC the table-directory lookup compares against, letting the CPAL accessor reach v1() with version!=0.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds carry different bytes at 0x0c-0x0f (e.g. 47/4f/53 'GOS...'), so the table-directory FOURCC comparison never matches 'CPAL' and the CPAL parse path is not entered.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted strcmp/memcmp of the input tag against the literal 'CPAL' FOURCC from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact 4 bytes at the matching offset; value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot plant the exact tag, so the I2S delta (vpc over value_profile) is what supplies this byte feature.

what_input_feature: The surrounding sfnt/CPAL header must be structurally valid enough (offset-table counts and CPAL version field non-zero) to pass sanitize and reach v1(); this is a multi-field gradient condition, not a single literal.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds begin 00 01 00 00 00 01 (valid sfnt version + numTables) and carry consistent table offset/length bytes (00 00 00 18 / 00 01 08 ...) so CPAL::sanitize succeeds (W hits get_size and sanitize while L does not), allowing version!=0 to be observed.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser cannot incrementally improve the multi-field header toward validity because it lacks the CMP_MAP distance feedback that preserves near-matching structural inputs; its blocked seeds never accumulate the consistent offset/length structure.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient buckets each integer CMP by operand-distance, preserving inputs whose header counts/lengths get closer to passing sanitize; this hill-climb on structural integer comparisons is absent in cmplog, so the value_profile delta (vpc over cmplog) supplies the structural-validity feature.

what_input_feature: Input must carry valid font-table magic/tag bytes (e.g. a 'typ1' sfnt version word and a 'loca' table tag) so the loca/glyf tables parse and num_glyphs becomes large enough that a chosen gid is in range, letting gid >= num_glyphs evaluate false.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner seeds open with the literal bytes 74 79 70 31 ('typ1') and contain 6c 6f 63 61 ('loca') at offset 0x0c — exact table-identifier byte sequences that I2SRandReplace plants from intercepted CMP operands so the table directory parses and num_glyphs is populated.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser lacks I2SRandReplace, so it never substitutes the exact 'typ1'/'loca' identifier bytes; its blocking seeds instead start with bytes like fe ff ff ff or e0 17 00 00 that do not form the table magic, leaving the glyph table unparsed and gid always >= num_glyphs (true branch).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds where the parser compared the input against the 'typ1'/'loca' identifiers, and substitutes the exact matching operand bytes into the input — value_profile has no such substitution stage, so vpc's I2S half is the technique that distinguishes the winner from the value_profile loser.

what_input_feature: Beyond the literal table tags, the input must drive the glyph-count / offset integer comparisons close enough to a valid in-range gid that the parser accepts the structure; this is the gradient-solvable numeric portion (num_glyphs and offset fields) rather than the exact-literal portion.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner additionally exercises the CMP_MAP gradient: its avg hitcount on the false-branch path is 2 vs 0 for the cmplog loser, and it reaches the in-range gid so get_points runs (W hits 2 on the enclosing function), indicating the Hamming/prefix-distance feedback steered the numeric fields toward a valid count.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback (it only buffers operands for I2S), so it cannot preserve inputs that get progressively closer on the num_glyphs/offset integer comparisons; it stays blocked 10/10 with avg hitcount 0 on the branch and never takes the false side.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP writes a (PC, operand-distance bucket) entry per CMP and rewards inputs whose operands are closer to matching, giving a hill-climbing gradient on the numeric glyph-count/offset comparisons that plain cmplog (I2S substitution only, no distance feedback) lacks — so vpc's value_profile half is what distinguishes the winner from the cmplog loser.

what_input_feature: A valid sfnt/OpenType container whose table directory names a 'CFF ' table (bytes 43 46 46 20 at the tag position) with a correctly-formed sfnt version and table offset, so this->blob->as<OT::cff1>() returns a real CFF table rather than the shared Null sentinel.
why_winner_satisfies: All four winner-resolving seeds (value_profile_cmplog, trial 1) carry the literal 'CFF ' (43 46 46 20) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f plus the sfnt magic 00 01 00 00 at 0x00-0x03 and a 00 00 00 18 table offset, exactly the byte set that lets the blob resolve to a non-Null OT::cff1 so line 1051 takes its false (winning) branch.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds (value_profile, trial 1) lack the 'CFF ' tag at that offset (W shows 43(C)x4/46(F)x4 there while L shows 47(G)/4f(O)/53(S) and scattered bytes), so the blob never resolves to a CFF table and line 1051 always takes its true branch into { fini (); return; }.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog wins because its I2SRandReplace stage reads the table-tag comparison from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact 'CFF ' operand into the input at the tag offset; value_profile alone lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot place the exact 4-byte tag, while cmplog alone fails to bank the partially-correct sfnt header progress, so only the combined fuzzer reaches the false branch.

function: hb-ot-font.cc:hb_ot_get_glyph_v_origin(hb_font_t*, void*, unsigned int, int*, int*, void*)
what_input_feature: A glyf-bearing font blob whose 4-byte table tags (e.g. 'loca'/'glyf') match the exact ASCII literals harfbuzz compares against, so the glyf accelerator parses and get_extents succeeds. Winner bytes carry 'typ1' at 0x00-0x03 and 'loca' at 0x0c-0x0f.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seeds (value_profile_cmplog) place 'typ1' at offset 0x00 and the literal 'loca' at offset 0x0c-0x0f, exact ASCII table tags that let the glyf table be located; the vpc lineage contains an I2S-floor (dash-row) ancestor that substituted such an operand.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile (loser of pair 2) has no I2SRandReplace mutator and never plants the exact 4-byte table tags; its blocking seeds carry no 'loca' tag and stay on the false branch (T=0 F=20).
mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operand buffer and substitutes the compared 4-byte table-tag literal into the input at the matched offset, planting 'loca'/'glyf' tags that value_profile cannot synthesize by havoc; this is the I2S axis that value_profile lacks.

function: hb-ot-font.cc:hb_ot_get_glyph_v_origin(hb_font_t*, void*, unsigned int, int*, int*, void*)
what_input_feature: Beyond the exact table tags, the glyf/loca offset and length fields must be progressively driven toward in-range values so glyf->get_extents resolves; a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient over those integer CMPs is needed.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner adds the CMP_MAP gradient on top of I2S: value_profile_cmplog resolves 10/10 while cmplog alone resolves 0/10, so the distance-bucket feedback that preserves near-matching offset operands is the marginal technique that completes the gate.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (loser of pair 1) has only edge-bucket feedback and no CMP_MAP gradient, so it cannot climb the multi-byte offset/length comparisons incrementally and stays blocked (loser resolved=0/10 blocked=10).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records (PC, operand-distance bucket); an input whose offset/length operand is closer to matching adds a new bucket and is kept as corpus, providing the gradient that lets the glyf offset converge — the technique cmplog lacks.

function: hb-ot-font.cc:hb_ot_draw_glyph(hb_font_t*, void*, unsigned int, hb_draw_funcs_t*, void*, void*)
what_input_feature: Exact byte literals identifying TrueType table tags / fixed sfnt header fields (e.g. the 'typ1'/'loca' tag bytes at offsets 0x00-0x0f) that string/integer CMPs in the table-directory parser compare against; these must match exactly before glyf->get_path can locate and walk a glyph outline and return true.
why_winner_satisfies: Both winner-resolving seeds carry the ASCII run 'typ1' at offsets 0x00-0x03 and 'loca' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, planted byte-for-byte, exactly the kind of operand that I2SRandReplace substitutes from the CMP buffer into the input.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser-blocking seeds carry no such tag run at those offsets (zeros, 0xe0, 0x4c, 'G'/'P'/'O'/'S' fragments instead), and value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator to copy the compared tag bytes into place, so it stays on the blocked false side for all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the tag/header bytes the parser compared against, and substitutes them at the matching input offsets; value_profile lacks this mutator and cannot plant the exact tag literals, so only the vpc winner (which has I2S) clears the pair.

function: hb-ot-font.cc:hb_ot_draw_glyph(hb_font_t*, void*, unsigned int, hb_draw_funcs_t*, void*, void*)
what_input_feature: Multi-byte offset/length fields in the table directory and loca/glyf headers that must reach jointly-consistent values for the glyph parser to take the true branch; these are wide integer comparisons better solved by gradient-style progressive matching than by single-shot substitution.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner seeds contain structured little-endian-looking dword fields (e.g. offsets 0x18-0x1f with 00 00 1f / 03) that a CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient can hill-climb toward, and vpc preserves each closer-matching intermediate as corpus.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog uses only edge-bucket feedback with no CMP_MAP gradient bucket, so partial progress on these wide multi-byte fields is not preserved as new coverage; cmplog stays blocked 10/10 with avg hitcount 0 on the branch.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records (PC, operand-distance bucket) and rewards inputs whose compared operands are closer to matching, giving a gradient over the wide offset/length fields; cmplog has no such gradient feedback, so vpc (which adds CMP_MAP) is needed to clear this pair over plain cmplog.

what_input_feature: A byte string that incrementally satisfies the SQL keyword/token comparisons feeding sqlite3NameFromToken, e.g. a near-complete 'PRAGMA <name>;' form where multi-byte keyword/identifier compares are progressively closer to matching.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds begin with the exact ASCII keyword 'PRAGMA' (50 52 41 47 4d 41) and a structured identifier/terminator tail, a token sequence the parser accepts so the blocker takes its false (resolving) side.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds are short malformed fragments ('SELEEC', '""/""') that fail keyword compares early; without the Hamming/prefix-distance CMP_MAP gradient, cmplog cannot retain and refine inputs that are only partially closer to a valid keyword, so it stays blocked 10/10.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching the SQL keyword/identifier comparisons; this gradient is the delta cmplog lacks, letting vpc climb toward the well-formed token stream that reaches the false branch.

what_input_feature: The literal SQL keyword bytes (e.g. 'PRAGMA') and structural punctuation that the lexer/parser compares against fixed string constants, placed at the offsets the comparisons read.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry the exact 'PRAGMA' literal plus a trailing identifier ('Ostor', 'vdbe_'); the winning vpc seed's ancestor chain contains an I2S-floor dash row, evidence that I2SRandReplace substituted a matched CMP operand into the input to produce the keyword.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile loser seeds are corrupt keyword fragments ('ac 45 4c 45 ...ELE', repeated ';;' runs) lacking the exact literal; without I2SRandReplace, value_profile cannot copy the parser's expected string constant directly into the input and stays blocked 10/10.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the comparison's other operand (the expected SQL keyword literal) into the input at the matched offset; this I2S substitution is the delta value_profile lacks, supplying the exact keyword bytes that drive the parser to the false branch.

what_input_feature: A PRAGMA statement whose name-token byte sequence progressively approaches a real pragma name in the case-insensitive name table scanned by pragmaLocate (offsets 0x00-0x08 carrying 'PRAGMA ' plus a pragma-name prefix).
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner seed is the well-formed string 'PRAGMA Ostor;-' (offsets 0x00-0x06 = 50 52 41 47 4d 41 20), driving pragmaLocate's sqlite3StrICmp name comparisons toward a match; vpc carries the CMP_MAP gradient feedback that rewards inputs whose name bytes get closer to a table entry.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser has I2S but no CMP_MAP gradient, so it gets no partial-progress reward as a candidate pragma name converges; its blocking seeds are non-PRAGMA garbage like 'SELEEC' and '""/""' that never reach the pragma-name compare loop, and it blocks all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets on the sqlite3StrICmp operand comparisons) preserves corpus inputs whose pragma-name token gets incrementally closer to a real table name; cmplog lacks this distance feedback, so it is the value_profile delta that separates vpc from cmplog.

what_input_feature: The exact pragma-name token bytes that equal a real entry in pragmaLocate's name table, so the StrICmp returns equal and the loop exits via the false (non-blocked-globally) path.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner's ancestor chain contains an I2S-floor (dash) row at depth 6, evidence that I2SRandReplace substituted the compared pragma-name operand into the input; vpc has the CmpLogObserver buffer that lets I2S copy the literal expected name bytes into the 'PRAGMA ' token region.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace stage, so it can only inch toward the name byte-by-byte and never lands the exact multi-byte token; it blocks 10/10 trials and its seeds (e.g. ';;\;;E<' and '.ELE.EL@E..CT') never form a valid pragma name.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operand recorded at the sqlite3StrICmp call and substitutes the exact expected pragma-name bytes into the input; value_profile lacks I2S, so the I2S delta is what separates vpc from value_profile.

what_input_feature: The input must carry the exact ASCII token 'PRAGMA' at offset 0x00 (bytes 50 52 41 47 4d 41) so the parser dispatches into sqlite3Pragma and reaches the pragma-name comparison at 125998:7.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed begins '50 52 41 47 4d 41 20 4f 73 74 6f 72' = 'PRAGMA Ostor', presenting the verbatim PRAGMA keyword that the loser seeds (all SELECT/junk) never form.
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed is a SELECT-like or junk byte run ('SELEEC', '""/""', '.ELE.EL@E..CT') with no 'PRAGMA' token, so the pragma-name branch at 125998 is never entered (L shows T=0 F=20).
mechanism_attribution: Against value_profile (Pair 2 delta=I2S), only the winner has I2SRandReplace, which reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the program's 'PRAGMA' keyword operand into the input at matching offsets; the winning seed's lineage carries an I2S-floor dash row at depth 6, evidence of an I2S find in its ancestry.

what_input_feature: Same feature: the literal 'PRAGMA' keyword (50 52 41 47 4d 41) at offset 0x00 gating entry into sqlite3Pragma and the true side of 125998:7.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed 'PRAGMA Ostor;-...' supplies the full keyword and reaches the branch (W shows T=1 F=1 at L125998), while cmplog's corpus stalls without it.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds ('SELEEC .`', '""/"" ') prefix-collide on no more than a couple of bytes with 'PRAGMA' and never accrete the full keyword, so they keep taking the false side (loser resolved=0/10, blocked=10).
mechanism_attribution: Against cmplog (Pair 1 delta=value_profile), only the winner adds the CMP_MAP gradient: each integer/string CMP writes a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket so an input whose prefix is closer to 'PRAGMA' than any seen before is preserved as corpus, giving a graded climb toward the keyword that plain cmplog edge-feedback lacks.

what_input_feature: The input must begin with the exact SQL keyword bytes 'PRAGMA' (50 52 41 47 4d 41) so the SQLite tokenizer emits the PRAGMA token that drives yy_reduce to this reduce action at 157969.
why_winner_satisfies: Both winner-resolving seeds carry the literal ASCII 'PRAGMA' at offsets 0x00-0x05 (PRAGMA Ostor / PRAGMA vdbe_), and only the winner takes the true branch (W T=2 F=17 vs L T=0 F=135 at L157969).
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile-discovered loser seeds are SELECT-family / punctuation garbage (e.g. ELE...CT, ;;\;;E<) with no contiguous PRAGMA keyword, so the tokenizer never emits the PRAGMA token and the true side stays at T=0.
mechanism_attribution: Adding I2S (cmplog's I2SRandReplace) to value_profile lets the mutator read the per-execution CMP buffer and substitute the keyword-matching bytes for 'PRAGMA' at the input head; the winning vpc seed's ancestor chain contains an I2S-floor dash row, confirming an I2S contribution that value_profile alone lacks.

what_input_feature: Same PRAGMA-keyword reduce target, but reaching it also requires progressively closing the comparison distance on the keyword/operand bytes so a near-match input is preserved as a corpus stepping stone.
why_winner_satisfies: Adding value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient to cmplog gives the winner the new-CMP_MAP-bucket feedback that retains partial-keyword inputs; the winner resolves 9/10 and reaches the branch (avg hitcount on branch winner=5), whereas plain cmplog does not.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog-only loser seeds (SELEEC, ""/"") are short corrupted fragments discovered at 1-81s that block 10/10 (loser resolved=0/10 blocked=10) — without the CMP_MAP gradient cmplog cannot accrete the partial-keyword corpus needed to assemble PRAGMA.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP; an input whose operand pair is closer to matching the PRAGMA comparison adds a new bucket and is kept as corpus, supplying I2S with a better substitution substrate that cmplog alone cannot retain.
